name: Lexicomp Drug Information API
what_it_does: Lexicomp (Wolters Kluwer) REST API for comprehensive clinical drug information. Enables AI agents to access drug monograph data including dosing, pharmacokinetics, and administration for clinical decision support, retrieve drug interaction severity and clinical significance data for medication safety checking, access drug allergy cross-reactivity data for contraindication screening, retrieve patient counseling and drug education content for patient communication, access IV compatibility data for pharmacy admixture decisions, handle drug identification by imprint, NDC, or trade name, retrieve pediatric and neonatal dosing data for specialized populations, access natural product and dietary supplement interaction data, retrieve toxicology and overdose management data, and integrate clinical drug intelligence with EHR, pharmacy, and clinical decision support systems.
use_cases: Checking drug interactions from clinical decision support agents
Retrieving dosing information from medication management agents
Screening drug allergies from patient safety agents
Accessing IV compatibility from pharmacy automation agents
not_for: Consumer drug lookup without clinical accuracy requirements
Non-clinical applications without licensed clinical drug data need
Administrative pharmacy (NDC pricing) without clinical drug content
best_when: A health system, pharmacy, or EHR vendor using Lexicomp clinical drug data wants AI agents to automate medication safety checks, dosing recommendations, drug interaction screening, and patient counseling content delivery.
avoid_when: PATIENT SAFETY RISK: Lexicomp drug interaction and dosing data is for clinical decision support — never auto-approve medication orders based solely on API results without pharmacist or prescriber review. Drug interaction severity classifications require clinical context (patient-specific factors override severity ratings). Pediatric dosing automation without weight-based calculation validation creates dosing errors. IV compatibility decisions require pharmacy validation beyond compatibility data alone.

auth.notes: Lexicomp uses API key and OAuth 2.0 authentication. Per-institution licensing. Part of Wolters Kluwer Health portfolio (acquired 2010). No webhooks — point-in-time drug data queries. REST API with JSON. Hudson, Ohio origin; now part of Wolters Kluwer global health information. Competes with Micromedex (Merative) and First Databank for clinical drug database market. Used by 4,500+ hospitals.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual institutional license
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual license by institution size, API call volume, and content modules
pricing.notes: Hudson, Ohio. Founded 1978. Acquired by Wolters Kluwer (2010). Part of Wolters Kluwer Health. 4,500+ hospitals. Lexicomp Online, Lexicomp Mobile, and Lexicomp API products. Strong clinical drug interaction and dosing content. Also offers dental and natural medicines content. Competes with Micromedex (IBM/Merative) and First Databank (Hearst) for hospital pharmacy CDS.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; institution and module dependent

known_agent_gotchas: PATIENT SAFETY RISK: Drug interaction severity is clinical context-dependent; automated contraindication blocking based solely on API severity classification without clinical context creates inappropriate alert fatigue or missed clinical actions
Content module licensing — Lexicomp content is modular (drug interactions, IV compatibility, natural medicines, dentistry); API access requires appropriate module license
Data update frequency — Lexicomp drug database updates weekly; cached drug data may miss recent safety updates; understand update cycle for your use case
Pediatric dosing complexity — weight-based dosing requires patient weight input and age-specific dosing table selection; validate calculation logic beyond raw API data
IV compatibility scope — Lexicomp IV compatibility data covers specific diluents and concentrations; compatibility decision requires matching clinical scenario to documented conditions
International vs. US content — Lexicomp content is primarily US-centric; international drug names and availability differ from US monographs
agent_readiness.security_notes: Clinical drug information. SOC2, HIPAA, ISO27001. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Patient medication safety data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Clinical drug data. SOC2, HIPAA. API key/OAuth2. US. Patient medication and drug safety data.
